$comment: Flow composition data. The substrate evaluates each clause's `when` against the open-context (asker, verb, place, being, time, world signals) at moment-assign. The first non-stacked clause whose `when` matches becomes the primary able; every stacked clause (`stack: true`) whose `when` matches contributes its capabilities and prompt body on top.

clauses.$comment: Always-on base. `stack: true` means this able's prompt + capabilities union into the composition regardless of which primary able matches.

A context-conditional clause. `when` is a small expression language: equality / membership / present / and / or / not over open-context keys. Without `stack`, the first matching clause becomes the primary.
Fallback primary when nothing else matches. No `when` means always-matches.
